content:
You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>SPIN按鈕按完會突然自己亮起又暗掉，幫我檢查原因</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-06-29</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

=======================

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). 1. First, they read a section of ExtraBetComponent.ts, which handles extra bet functionality and animations. The code shows various animation states (ExtraBet_ON, ExtraBet_OFF, ExtraBet_Active) and state management with SetSpinState calls.

2. Then they searched for "SpinReqNotify" events across the codebase and found 3 occurrences in GameView.ts at lines 1151, 1246, and 1475.

This looks like they're investigating the SPIN button behavior, likely looking at where spin requests are triggered and how the button state changes. The ExtraBetComponent code shows multiple places where `SetSpinState` is called with `SlotBottomBarState.CLICK_SPIN` and `SlotBottomBarState.IDLE`, which could be related to the button lighting/dimming issue.

This is a discovery phase - they're reading code to understand how the SPIN button state is controlled. I should record this as an observation about what they're discovering about the system.

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>SPIN button state management investigation</title>
  <subtitle>Investigating SPIN button unexpected lighting/dimming behavior through ExtraBetComponent and SpinReqNotify events</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>ExtraBetComponent.ts contains multiple SetSpinState calls toggling between CLICK_SPIN and IDLE states</fact>
    <fact>SpinReqNotify event is dispatched from three locations in GameView.ts (lines 1151, 1246, 1475)</fact>
    <fact>ExtraBetComponent animations (ExtraBet_ON, ExtraBet_OFF, ExtraBet_Active) manage m_ExtraBetAniPlaying flag during state transitions</fact>
    <fact>SetSpinState(SlotBottomBarState.CLICK_SPIN) is called before animations, SetSpinState(SlotBottomBarState.IDLE) after animations complete</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Investigation began into SPIN button unexpectedly lighting up and dimming after being pressed. The ExtraBetComponent manages extra bet functionality with multiple animation states and uses SetSpinState to control the bottom bar's SPIN button state. During extra bet mode changes and bar extend/collapse animations, the code sets the button to CLICK_SPIN state (likely disabling it), then back to IDLE after animations complete. Three SpinReqNotify events were found in GameView.ts that may be triggering spin requests. The lighting/dimming issue may be caused by state transitions during these animation sequences or event dispatches.</narrative>

Now I see that there's an OnSpin() method in ExtraBetComponent that calls ExtendShowBar(false) when spin is triggered. This is additional context about how the spin button interacts with the extra bet bar animation system.

This is a discovery observation - learning more about how the system works and potentially finding the cause of the issue.

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>OnSpin triggers ExtraBet bar collapse animation</title>
  <subtitle>OnSpin method in ExtraBetComponent calls ExtendShowBar(false) which triggers animation state changes</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>OnSpin() method at line 279 calls ExtendShowBar(false) to collapse the extra bet bar</fact>
    <fact>ExtendShowBar(false) sets m_ExtraBetAniPlaying flag and toggles SetSpinState during animation playback</fact>
    <fact>Comment at line 298 indicates "相當於按下spin 所以也要縮bar" (equivalent to pressing spin, so bar must collapse)</fact>
    <fact>ExtendShowBar animations include ExtraBet_Close followed by either ExtraBet_Active or ExtraBet_OFF state</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The OnSpin() callback triggers ExtendShowBar(false) which initiates the extra bet bar collapse animation sequence. During this animation, the code sets the spin button state to CLICK_SPIN (disabled) while animations play, then resets to IDLE after completion. This state management during the collapse animation is likely causing the SPIN button to light up (become enabled) and dim (become disabled) unexpectedly after being pressed, as the bar collapse animation runs in response to the spin action.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>extensions/astarte-framework/assets/Component/ExtraBetComponent.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```

//=============================================================\\n    \\n    //=============================================================\\n    //#region ConnectionManager\\n    // ConnectionManager需實作 OnCommand、GetSpinInfo、CancelSpin\\n    OnCommand( type: number, data: Uint8Array )\\n    {\\n\\n        switch ( type )\\n        {\\n            case Define.AckType.info:\\n                {\\n\\n                    eventManager.Dispatch(GAME_INTRO_MANAGER_EVENT.INIT, this);\\n\\n                    eventManager.Dispatch(REEL_UI_MANAGER_EVENT.INIT, this);\\n\\n ExtraBet（gate 集中於 GameView 各入口方法）\\n                    this.m_showExtra = info.ShowExtra ?? false;\\n\\n                    this.SetTxt();\\n                    this.SettingBottomBar();\\n\\n                     // 發送更新Logo圖片事件\\n                     eventManager.Dispatch(REEL_UI_MANAGER_EVENT.UPDATE_LOGO);\\n                    \\n                    newBottombarManager.AutoPlayPanelInit();\\n                    newExtraManager.FeaturesInit(\\n                        [ \\\"features/left/spriteFrame\\\", \\\"features/right/spriteFrame\\\" ],\\n                        [ Game_Define.StringKey.MC_FEATURES_LEFT, Game_Define.StringKey.MC_FEATURES_RIGHT ],\\n                        Game_Define.SPICY_LEVEL,\\n                        this.m_titleSpriteAtlas\\n                    );\\n                    let atlas: SpriteAtlas = localeStringManager.GetGameAtlas();\\n                    newExtraManager.SetFeatruesDesc( [ atlas.getSpriteFrame( \\\"txt1\\\" ), atlas.getSpriteFrame( \\\"txt2\\\" ) ] );\\n                    newExtraManager.CheckFirstFeatures();\\n                    \\n                    // TODO: 先註解掉，改用公版BuyBonus\\n\\n                    // 設置Extra Bet倍率資訊\\n                    newBottombarManager.SetMultiBetInfo({\\n                        IsMulti: () => this.m_showExtra && ( this.m_extraBetComp?.IsExtra ?? false ),\\n                        Multi: info.Mul\\n                    });\\n                    this.m_betMultipliers = info.Mul.slice();\\n                    const extraBetMultiplierRates: number[] = info.Mul.slice(1);\\n                    this.m_extraBetWindow?.Init( extraBetMultiplierRates );\\n\\n                    newBottombarManager.SetSpinState( SlotBottomBarState.IDLE );\\n\\n                    newBottombarManager.SetTxt();\\n                    newExtraManager.SetTxt();\\n                    this.OnRotation();\\n                    newBottombarManager.SetAutoPlayPanelTxt();\",\"numLines\":95,\"startLine\":955,\"totalLines\":2341}}"</outcome>

content.thinking:
This is showing the OnCommand method in GameView.ts, specifically the info case where the game initializes. I can see at line 1043 there's a call to `newBottombarManager.SetSpinState( SlotBottomBarState.IDLE );`. This is part of the initialization process.

Let me wait for more information about where the actual problem is occurring. The user is investigating why the SPIN button lights up and dims unexpectedly after being pressed. We've seen:
1. ExtraBetComponent has OnSpin() that calls ExtendShowBar(false)
2. ExtendShowBar sets spin state to CLICK_SPIN during animations, then back to IDLE
3. SpinReqNotify events are dispatched from GameView.ts

Now we're looking at initialization code. Let me wait for more relevant findings about the actual bug.

content.text: I'm observing the initialization flow. Waiting for more findings related to the SPIN button lighting/dimming issue...
